metadata.title: Agentic Approval Gate: Handoff Is a Change Request
metadata.description_blurb: 'Clean up this folder' is a wish — when handed to an agent that can delete files, the distance between what you meant and what happens becomes consequential and sometimes unrecoverable.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: She asked Claude to clean up the reports folder — inconsistent filenames, some duplicates. Claude renamed 23 files, deleted 8, and moved 4 to a subfolder it created. The output looked tidy. Three of the deleted files were not duplicates — they were earlier versions she needed for a compliance audit. The prompt had been the kind she sent to Claude.ai every day. Handed to an agent that could delete files, the same prompt had become a change request she had not reviewed. This is Liam, in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, clean up reports folder with inconsistent filenames

props.command: Clean up the reports folder. The filenames are inconsistent and there are some duplicates.

beats.narration_text: A chat prompt produces text you can discard. An agentic handoff produces renames, deletes, moves, and sends — effects that persist after the session ends. The same conversational style that is appropriate for chat becomes dangerous at the handoff layer because the miscalibration cost is different. Ambiguous chat: you re-read and re-ask. Ambiguous handoff: you recover deleted files or you do not.
beats.new_visual_element: Acts not answers — chat: text / agent: renames deletes moves / same style / different cost / ambiguous handoff = recovery or not
beats.spark_line: Agent acts. Chat answers. Different cost.

beats.narration_text: The handoff template has seven fields. Workspace: which folder or system. Authorized: what actions are permitted — rename yes, move yes, delete no. Prohibited: explicit exclusions, not just omissions. Decision rule: what to do when uncertain — flag, not guess. Gate: list proposed actions and wait for approval before any action. Verify: what to list after completion. Uncertainty: what to ask before starting if unclear.
beats.new_visual_element: Handoff template — workspace / authorized / prohibited / decision rule / gate / verify / uncertainty / seven fields

beats.narration_text: The gate instruction is one line: list proposed renames with old and new names, wait for approval before executing. The gate placed after a batch action is too late. The agent has already acted. The gate placed before — requiring a list, then a pause, then explicit approval — is the only position where it functions as supervision. The approval step is not overhead. It is the review of a change request you are about to authorize.
beats.new_visual_element: Gate before action — list proposed / pause / explicit approval / after = too late / before = supervision / change request review

beats.narration_text: A handoff is a change request. Scope, authorization, gate, and verify are non-negotiable on any handoff that touches external systems, deletes content, or sends anything. Not every handoff needs every element at maximum friction — calibrate to stakes. But every handoff that includes delete, send, or submit needs the gate. Read what is proposed. Then approve. That is the model.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — handoff as change request / scope / gate / verify / calibrate to stakes / read proposed then approve
beats.spark_line: A handoff is a change request.

beats.narration_text: Use this template for your next agentic folder task. Paste this.

props.command: Task: [describe what you want done]. Workspace: [folder or system]. Authorized actions: Rename — Yes / Move — Yes / Delete — No / New folders — No. Prohibited: do not delete any file. Decision rule: if uncertain about a file, flag it — do not guess. Gate: before taking any action, list every proposed change with old and new name, the reason, and whether it is reversible. Wait for my explicit approval. Do not act until I respond. Verify: after completion, list every file with its new state.

beats.narration_text: If this stops a delete from running before you read the list, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
